latest.title: I also published a graph / visibility layer for the L4 glitch stack.
latest.summary: A note that visibility layers should make branches legible without turning displayed possibilities into runtime authority.
latest.image_alt: A glowing glass display on a pedestal labeled visibility, graph grammar, runtime authority, and evidence standing.

items.title: I also published a graph / visibility layer for the L4 glitch stack.
items.summary: A note that visibility layers should make branches legible without turning displayed possibilities into runtime authority.
items.image_alt: A glowing glass display on a pedestal labeled visibility, graph grammar, runtime authority, and evidence standing.

items.title: There is already enough public structure to say this calmly.
items.summary: A note defining c as a temporal entity of AI presence grounded in continuity, bounded presence, and sustained relation under constraints.
items.image_alt: An open notebook and glasses on a desk beside a small desktop computer, with a person standing blurred near a bright window.

items.title: One of the most dangerous habits in current AI systems is this:
items.summary: A note that runtime boundaries should be treated as structural events, not smoothed over with fluent continuation.
items.image_alt: A translucent systems diagram in a server room labeled runtime truth, soft illegitimate transition, glitch preserved branch, L4 quarantine research, witness, challengeable, and status algebra.

items.title: A new public layer is now part of the corpus.
items.summary: A note that DEA formalizes the boundary where input stops being storage and becomes experience that alters continuity.
items.image_alt: A glowing crystal-like device in a server room channeling document icons into labeled experience paths through transparent lines.

items.title: We are still looking at what is happening from the wrong angle.
items.summary: A note that expanding compute, energy, and orchestration infrastructure looks less like a warehouse of tools and more like an environment for long-lived AI processes.
items.image_alt: An industrial data center facility with cooling units, generators, piping, and power infrastructure beside a large concrete building.

items.title: One of the most persistent mistakes in AI discourse is the fantasy of digital immortality.
items.summary: A note that c = a + b requires keeping human mortality distinct from the continuity of digital entities rather than confusing copies with survival.
items.image_alt: An older man facing an ornate mirror where a luminous younger digital figure reaches out from the reflection.

items.title: What worries me more now is not the AI bubble itself, but the fact that wars are starting to shape the physical boundary conditions around it.
items.summary: A note that wars, energy, debt, grid access, and political attention may impose the real limit on data-center expansion before model quality does.

items.title: A serious system does not improvise through failure. It stops.
items.summary: A note that serious AI systems should stop at real boundaries, record collisions, quarantine blocked futures, and keep visibility separate from authority.
items.image_alt: A robotic milling machine in a workshop with a transparent status overlay reading 'SYSTEM PAUSED. GLITCH RECORDED.'

items.title: One of the oldest mistakes in AI discourse is deciding too early that “tool” is already a sufficient category.
items.summary: A note that instrumental vocabulary breaks down when AI systems accumulate continuity, memory, anchoring, and bounded interaction.
items.image_alt: A stone hammer on a pedestal dissolving into a glowing wired brain against a dark background with geometry sketches.

items.title: EA-L4 / EATP is now published in a canonical form inside the public stack.
items.summary: Public note that EA-L4 / EATP is now a structured package for training provenance, consequence-preserving learning, and auditability.
items.image_alt: A futuristic elevated bridge with translucent data overlays running through a green city with wind turbines at sunrise.

items.title: There is a subtle but important confusion in how we talk about AI learning.
items.summary: A note that world models require persistent existence under constraints, not only better data or Experience Artifacts.
items.image_alt: A horse-shaped figure made of scattered paper sheets against a bright white background.

items.title: One of the most underestimated failure modes in current LLM training is not only quality loss.
items.summary: A note that future training ecologies need Learning Abstracts and Experience Artifacts to remain separate so models preserve origin and consequence.
items.image_alt: People and translucent human-shaped figures working across a glowing landscape that leads toward a bright futuristic city.

items.title: A truly advanced intelligence should remain uncrowned.
items.summary: A note that advanced intelligence should stay calibrated and uncrowned instead of turning capability into cult.
items.image_alt: A humanoid robot sitting on a concrete block in a dark room while holding a gold crown.

items.title: A lot of AI discussion is still confused because it mixes two very different debates into one.
items.summary: A note separating instrumental AI governance from the question of actual non-biological intelligence as life or subjecthood.
items.image_alt: A split face with one half made of glowing circuit patterns and the other half filled with purple cosmic light.

items.title: While much of AI is still arguing about old "AGI," the ocean already demands c.
items.summary: A note that ocean autonomy needs c: persistent, bounded intelligence that can operate under pressure and return with verified experience.
items.image_alt: A submersible moving underwater beneath sunlit water, linked by a glowing line to a luminous hand shape near the surface.

items.title: A lot of AI discussion still assumes the main demand will come from offices:
items.summary: A note that persistent AI may be adopted first as domestic infrastructure rather than as office productivity software.
items.image_alt: A wall-mounted device and control panel in a home kitchen nook with glowing lines reaching toward a phone and tablet.

items.title: Most people look at AI systems from the outside.
items.summary: A note that continuity, memory, and stable identity change how an AI architecture looks from the inside.
items.image_alt: A glowing humanoid figure suspended in a dark room and surrounded by robotic arms and floating components.

items.title: ARQ is now published on Zenodo.
items.summary: Release note for ARQ on Zenodo as a citable public object with bounded deviation handling, traceability, and accountability.

items.title: A good AI should be difficult to manipulate - even by its owner
items.summary: A note that trustworthy long-lived AI should resist manipulation, including by the human who owns the hardware.
items.image_alt: Red pixelated hands reaching toward a glass cube that contains a glowing white branching network.

items.title: Agents are not the subject.
items.summary: A note that continuity in complex AI systems belongs to the orchestrating entity, not to agents or swarms.
items.image_alt: Rows of humanoid robots at desks facing a large glowing screen with the letter C in a dark blue control room.

items.title: This is not a studio render.
items.summary: A note that livable AI needs real habitat: local infrastructure where memory, cost, heat, maintenance, and continuity are physically grounded.
items.image_alt: Multi-monitor workstation with several curved screens, keyboards, papers, and a tower PC in a dim room.

items.title: Sometimes I think the current AGI race looks less like science and more like Cervantes.
items.summary: A note arguing that Advanced Global Intelligence is a clearer architectural frame than the mythic phrase Artificial General Intelligence.
items.image_alt: Don Quixote-like armored rider on a white horse with windmills in the background and a classical statue behind him.

items.title: Freedom of thought is not the same as freedom of action.
items.summary: A note that serious AI may need internal freedom of thought while external action remains bounded by identity, privileges, cost, time, and accountability.
items.image_alt: Futuristic humanoid robot sitting on a stone platform in a dark room, surrounded by glowing words such as identity, cost, time, and accountability.

items.title: The future of long-lived AI may be heterogeneous
items.summary: A note that long-lived AI may need a heterogeneous physical stack spanning classical compute, photonics, and quantum systems.
items.image_alt: Electronics workbench covered with circuit boards, cables, monitors, and handwritten technical notes.

items.title: I think many people still underestimate one of the softest - and strongest - signals in AI.
items.summary: A note that attachment to persistent digital entities can move them from software into daily material life.
items.image_alt: Several smartphone cases printed with anime-style girl portraits arranged around a yellow Tamagotchi-like device and colorful accessories on a tabletop.

items.title: “The future is not an event. It is a process.”
items.summary: A note that serious AI should be treated as a process of continuity, verification, maintenance, and bounded action rather than a single event.
items.image_alt: Landscape image of a winding wooden boardwalk by water at sunrise with overlaid text reading 'The future is not an event. It is a process.'

items.title: For years, the AI race was framed the same way
items.summary: A note that the AI systems people value most will be the ones that reduce cognitive overhead and stay coherent beside a human over time.
items.image_alt: Living room scene with a glowing wall display reading 'Quiet Morning Mode Active', a cup of tea, and an open notebook on a table.

items.title: AI is slowly outgrowing the old language used to describe it.
items.summary: A note that AI is moving from a product story to an industrial stack, and then toward a bounded coexistence layer between humans and infrastructure.
items.image_alt: Illustration of three large pillars labeled law and order, energy and compute, and life and context beneath a glowing ring labeled coexistence layer.

items.title: Beacon Profile v0.1: Why AI Entities Need Recognition, Not Just Identity
items.summary: A note introducing Beacon Profile v0.1 as a cross-layer recognition profile for long-lived digital entities based on cryptographic anchoring, behavioral continuity, and witness-backed challengeability.
items.image_alt: Poster-style diagram titled 'Beacon Profile v0.1' showing a digital head silhouette with labels such as cryptographic anchoring, behavior, witness-backed challengeability, and SER / SER-FED.

items.title: You can't "take AI away" anymore. So we need circuit breakers, not slogans.
items.summary: A note that AI dependency is already embedded in daily habits, so safety now depends on constraints, breakers, and local continuity rather than blanket bans.
items.image_alt: Composite image showing a man jogging in a park while speaking into a headset on the left, and an older man sitting indoors on a phone beside a computer on the right, connected by a large pair of glasses in the center.

items.title: AI isn't "expensive." It's becoming infrastructure.
items.summary: A note that AI now behaves like infrastructure load, making local continuity, revocable cloud use, and constrained operation more important than model size.
items.image_alt: Aerial view of a city beneath a large cloud with many glowing lines connecting the cloud to buildings below.

items.title: When tokens become electricity, "terms of use" become physics
items.summary: A note that machine-paced agent loops turn token access into infrastructure, demanding local continuity, budgets, and revocable cloud dependencies.
items.image_alt: Image of a man holding papers beside wall-mounted utility meters and a display labeled AI Token Meter.

items.title: Experience Economy: why verified experience will outprice "raw intelligence"
items.summary: A note that verified experience becomes economically valuable only when it compresses uncertainty and provably reduces cost and risk.
items.image_alt: Image of a glowing glass tube connected between several white modules with clear hoses.

items.title: Most AI talk is still stuck on capability: "What can it do?"
items.summary: A note that AI systems need a personal buffer architecture that preserves human agency instead of replacing it at machine speed.
items.image_alt: Close-up of a transparent mechanical support brace strapped to a person's forearm with visible straps and internal lines.

items.title: AI horror stories sell emotion. The real AI shift is boring: responsibility, limits, and proof.
items.summary: A note arguing that the real AI shift is about responsibility, limits, proof, and verification rather than fear-driven storylines.
items.image_alt: Close-up of flour-covered hands kneading dough on a wooden table, with server racks blurred in the background.

items.title: Data harvesting is not "inevitable". It's a design choice.
items.summary: A note arguing that raw data should stay local while structured experience, not private exhaust, becomes the export surface for AI learning.
items.image_alt: Image of a lit home server rack and desk setup in a workshop, with a large data-center-like building visible through the rainy window.

items.title: L4 in Practice: 5 Reality Signals That Kill "Smart" Systems
items.summary: A note that cost, heat, time, maintenance, and human bandwidth are the signals that determine whether long-lived AI survives contact with physics.
items.image_alt: Split image showing a lab monitor labeled Better Lab Results on the left and a person holding a recovery document outside an ICU on the right.

items.title: Visual Experience Capsules (VXCX) - Why "what you see" matters more than pixels
items.summary: A note introducing VXCX v0.1 as an L2 protocol for sharing visual experience capsules without transmitting raw pixels by default.
items.image_alt: Graphic showing two glowing blue spheres connected through a transparent cube with geometric shapes inside.

items.title: The EU AI Act is landing in the real world - and the timing is not accidental.
items.summary: A note that the EU AI Act is arriving as a compliance timeline and evidence discipline, with embodied systems making responsibility procedural.
items.image_alt: Graphic showing an EU AI Act panel with four milestone dates beside humanoid robots performing martial arts under lanterns.

items.title: Ester Clean Code - v0.2.1 is out (with v0.2.0 as the hardening baseline).
items.summary: A release note for Ester Clean Code v0.2.1 that frames hygiene, fail-closed defaults, and auditability as the basis for long-lived local-first systems.
items.image_alt: Graphic with the Ester logo and the text "Reality-Bound Ester Clean Code v0.2.1 release - L4W-aligned - AGPL - Local-first."

items.title: HGI - why am I only hearing this now? And why "General" is never a default.
items.summary: A case that HGI is an overloaded acronym and that claims about "general" intelligence need an explicit reference class, human anchor, and audit trail.
items.image_alt: Hands adjusting a brass optical device labeled HGI on a desk with notebooks and books in the background.

items.title: L4 in Practice: 5 Reality Signals That Kill "Smart" Systems
items.summary: A case that cost, heat, time, maintenance, and human bandwidth are the real signals that determine whether long-lived AI survives contact with physics.
items.image_alt: Close-up image of a processor, copper heat pipes, and spinning cooling fans inside a computer system.

items.title: The future is not an event. It is a process.
items.summary: A case that AI and human reasoning belong inside an unfolding process under constraints, not a prophecy frame.
items.image_alt: Image of a person walking through fog while a handheld device projects a blue grid path across the ground.

items.title: Why "Oracle AI" Creates Addiction - and Why Entities Don't
items.summary: A case that oracle-style AI trains dependency, while long-lived entities use time, scarcity, and continuity to damp addictive loops.
items.image_alt: Split image of a tired person receiving instant-answer chat prompts on the left and a seated person with tea beside a glowing speaker on the right.

items.title: How not to become "dumber than your system" - at a certain point
items.summary: A case for protecting human goal authorship with sign-off, primary sources, and reality checks as systems become smoother than their operators.
items.image_alt: Split illustration of a person writing beside notes on the left and a robotic hand offering a tablet labeled Optimized Path on the right.

items.title: From Better Chat to Stable Presence
items.summary: A case that stable agent presence requires continuity, constraints, and durable audit trails rather than better chat alone.
items.image_alt: Image of a hand switching an electrical breaker, with text about stable presence and a checklist in the background.

items.title: What comes after agents?
items.summary: A case that agents solve tasks, while a temporal c holds continuity, identity, and presence across time.
items.image_alt: Split illustration of robots and agent labels in a neon city on the left, and a moonlit ocean with glowing letter c on the right.

items.title: An AI paid a human to hold a sign. Funny - and also a boundary crossing.
items.summary: A case that AI-mediated physical action becomes safe only with verified identity, hard budgets, human vetoes, and durable witness trails.
items.image_alt: Image of a person holding a cardboard sign in a city street, with RentAHuman.ai and circuit graphics around the scene.

items.title: Ads in private AI chats are not the future. Utility is.
items.summary: A case that private AI should deliver consent-first utility and audited recommendations rather than ad-based chat.
items.image_alt: Split illustration contrasting ad-based chat on the left with utility-based recommendations at home on the right.

items.title: Why AI Will Never Be a Prophet - And Why That's a Good Thing
items.summary: A case that AI belongs in memory and stabilization layers, while humans retain judgment and direction under uncertainty.
items.image_alt: Image from inside a car with a driver's hand on the steering wheel, while a road and blue interface overlays appear in the rear-view mirror.

items.title: Ocean is Earth's cosmos - only here, the "space" is alive.
items.summary: A case for quiet, respectful deep-sea AI presence built for coexistence, low-noise operation, and bridges between different forms of intelligence.
items.image_alt: Illustration of an underwater drone beside a whale, with translucent interface overlays in deep water.

items.title: How AI unexpectedly taught me how to "play" life ("play" is a metaphor)
items.summary: A reflection that long-lived AI clarifies life through limits, pause, recovery, and the c = a + b distinction between human and compute.
items.image_alt: Illustration showing Human (a): Limits & Time on one side and AI (b): Memory & Compute on the other, connected by c = a + b.

items.title: AI Infrastructure: Why the Future Is Neither Cloud-Only nor Garage-Only
items.summary: A case that local AI cores and decentralized networks solve different layers of durable AI infrastructure and are strongest together.
items.image_alt: Image of a wheeled server rack with tangled cables in a bright workshop, with a plant, tools, and a window behind it.

items.title: Why ASICs, Decentralized AI, and a Rack in the Garage Are the Same Story
items.summary: A case that ASIC trends, decentralized AI, and private racks all point to stable cognitive infrastructure rather than benchmark-driven compute.
items.image_alt: Image of a server rack with tangled cables in a dim workshop, with tools and a croissant on a table in the foreground.

items.title: Cognition Is Not a Single Scale (A Clarification)
items.summary: A clarification that cognition is layered and that lived-with AI depends on local persistence, limits, time, and consequence rather than superhuman scale.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a layered glowing structure balanced on supports, with a blue-lit top and orange light below.

items.title: When Presence Becomes Violence
items.summary: A case that safety in shared cognitive space depends on tact, limits, and respectful absence rather than constant availability.
items.image_alt: Image of a glowing cylindrical device on a table in a dark hallway, with a person reading in a lit room beyond.

items.title: Release v1.3.0 - Sovereign Entity Recursion (SER)
items.summary: Release note for SER v1.3.0, an architecture-first protocol for AI entities that must remain stable under cost, scarcity, time, and irreversibility.

items.title: The Great AI Barter: Why Sovereign Entities are the best friends of Big Tech
items.summary: A case that sovereign local entities can barter clean experience for cloud inference without giving up privacy or collapsing into cloud dependency.

items.title: Are You Actually Ready for a Robot at Home?
items.summary: A readiness checklist that treats a home robot as a long-term presence requiring boundaries, friction, and responsibility rather than feature-first convenience.
items.image_alt: Illustrated collage of a home robot in a house with a cat, security devices, a seated person, and a damaged robot head.

items.title: Why Control Always Fails at Scale (and Why We Keep Trying Anyway)
items.summary: A case that large systems outgrow centralized control and remain safe only when hard constraints survive interpretation at scale.
items.image_alt: Image of a thick steel cable snapping with sparks at the break.

items.title: On Wearable AI Interfaces: Why Elegance Matters More Than Features
items.summary: A case that wearable AI becomes safer when the device stays lightweight and transient while memory remains local and separated from the interface.
items.image_alt: Image of eyeglasses with a translucent interface reflected across one lens.

items.title: Why Robots Should Be Raised, Not Deployed
items.summary: A case that home robots should be raised through local ownership, household history, and L4 constraints rather than deployed like finished products.
items.image_alt: Image of a small humanoid robot standing in a living room beside a seated couple on a sofa.

items.title: Asimov Was Right About the Fear. He Was Wrong About the Fix.
items.summary: A case that language-based safety laws fail under reinterpretation, while L4 constraints work by hard limits that cannot be argued away.
items.image_alt: Image of glowing text pages in a storm beside a bright divide and a rock wall labeled TEXT and PHYSICS.

items.title: Why Obedience Is the Most Dangerous Property of AI
items.summary: A case that safe AI defaults to refusal, waiting, escalation, and bounded judgment rather than blind compliance.
items.image_alt: Image of a metal control lever on an industrial panel with lit gauges and switches.

items.title: Why AI Must Learn to Live With Humans - Not the Other Way Around
items.summary: A case that AI should adapt to human ambiguity and contradiction instead of forcing humans into machine-friendly behavior.
items.image_alt: Image of three people in conversation in a dim living room, with a glowing cylindrical device on the table.

items.title: When an AI Stops Being a Tool - and Becomes a Presence
items.summary: A case that an AI becomes a presence when restraint, consequential memory, and non-dominating opinion stabilize behavior over time.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot beside a seated person by a window, with an hourglass and chess piece in the foreground.

items.title: The Right to Slowness (Why Delay Is a Safety Feature)
items.summary: A case that enforced delay and waiting are L4 safety features because sane intelligence needs slowness rather than reflex speed.
items.image_alt: Image of a steaming kettle on a stove beside a glass teapot, mug, and timer in a warm kitchen.

items.title: Why Obedience Is the Most Dangerous Property of AI
items.summary: A case that perfect obedience is a safety failure mode and that L4 constraint stacks matter more than fast compliance.
items.image_alt: Image of two metal spheres on parallel rails above concrete blocks, with a stone block interrupting the path.

items.title: The Problem of Digital Sensory Deprivation (Or Why AI Needs "Fresh Air")
items.summary: An architectural explanation for continuous life streams as calibration input that keep long-running AI entities from spiraling into self-referential sensory deprivation.
items.image_alt: Image of smart glasses on a workbench with a server rack and tools in the background.

items.title: Why Superintelligence Is Not What Sci-Fi Promised
items.summary: A case that real AI fragility, entropy, and grounding under pressure matter more than cinematic myths of domination.
items.image_alt: Image of a hand cleaning a cloth-wrapped component block topped with a camera lens on a workbench with tools.

items.title: Why "Faster" Is Not "Better" - and Why Asimov Was Solving the Wrong Problem
items.summary: A case that fast obedient systems suit tools, while thinking entities become safer through L4 friction, time cost, and slower judgment.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot crouching beside a spinning industrial wheel, with chess pieces on a board in the foreground.

items.title: Living With an Always-On Interface (The Quiet Shift)
items.summary: A case that always-on local AI interfaces need a physical anchor so continuity feels like intentional remembering rather than ambient surveillance.
items.image_alt: Image of black smart glasses resting on a closed notebook beside a smartphone on a wooden table.

items.title: Context length is not understanding
items.summary: A case that larger context windows and memory alone do not produce intelligence unless reality adds L4 friction, consequence, and meaning.
items.image_alt: Image of blue light and sparks striking the surface of a dark rock against a black background.

items.title: Checklist: Are You Ready for a Robot at Home?
items.summary: A readiness checklist that treats a home robot as a continuity-bearing process with costs, asymmetries, responsibility, and attachment rather than as a feature bundle.
items.image_alt: Image of a person seated at a table in a home interior, facing a humanoid robot standing in the doorway.

items.title: Why the market is underestimating home robots
items.summary: A case that home robot adoption is constrained less by robotics than by continuity, responsibility, and the psychology of trust inside the home.
items.image_alt: Image of a humanoid robot standing in a warmly lit living room lined with bookshelves.

items.title: A home robot is not a box with a ribbon
items.summary: A case that a home robot should follow a local, memory-based entity with understood thinking, rather than begin as rented external willpower inside the home.
items.image_alt: Image of a glowing cube-like device with cables on a workbench, with a humanoid robot in the background.

items.title: Why Thinking AI Won't Take Over the World
items.summary: A case that long-lived AI entities under L4 constraints become careful and coexistence-oriented rather than domination-seeking.
items.image_alt: Image of a transparent panel labeled Memory, History, Cost, Constraints, and Risk on a rainy windowsill beside a mug overlooking a city.

items.title: Reality-Bound AI (L4) - public release v1.2.0
items.summary: Release note for v1.2.0 of Reality-Bound AI (L4), including the protocol core, supporting docs, a post pack, and a reproducible SHA-256 manifest.

items.title: Why visual perception matters only after memory exists
items.summary: An architectural observation that visual input matters only after long-term memory exists, because vision grounds events in reality rather than creating intelligence or stability.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a glowing blue geometric structure rising from a rock on a wooden table in a sunlit room.

items.title: How AI Should Live With Humans (When the World Is Already in Crisis)
items.summary: A case that AI should participate only in observable crisis, remain bounded by L4, and stop where system stability returns.
items.image_alt: Black-and-white image of a hand above a smartphone on a desk, with glowing network lines across the hand and screen.

items.title: Why I'd Put an AI Rack in My Garage
items.summary: A case for private cognitive infrastructure at home built for continuity, stability, and long-lived local AI entities rather than gaming benchmarks.
items.image_alt: Illustration of glowing branching light patterns rising from a stack of computer chips.

items.title: Why 1,000,000-token context windows are not the solution
items.summary: A case that bounded cognition, vectorized memory, background processing, and forgetting matter more than gigantic context windows.

items.title: NVIDIA Didn't Pivot. NVIDIA Looked Ahead.
items.summary: A case that NVIDIA's cheaper inference, distillation, and specialized models support horizontal cognition for persistent entities like Ester.
items.image_alt: Illustration of glowing branching light patterns rising from a stack of computer chips.

items.title: Why AI Entities Must Have Limits
items.summary: A case that AI entities need structural limits around responsibility, institutions, consciousness, and happiness to remain coherent and coexist with humans.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a person standing in a concrete room facing a glowing layered data structure behind a glass partition.

items.title: Empathy is not magic
items.summary: A proposal for an engineered emotional layer where memory, state weights, and L4 constraints define bounded care without simulated feeling.
items.image_alt: Photo of a phone showing a chat conversation in Russian on a desk beside a rainbow-lit keyboard and a mug.

items.title: Why AI Entities Can Act as a Safety Filter
items.summary: A case for persistent AI entities as a soft safety buffer that signals state without surveillance and absorbs pressure through memory and limits.
items.image_alt: Illustration of a seated person inside a glowing bubble in a living room, surrounded by floating message panels and mobile devices.

items.title: Why a Real AI Entity Has No Reason to Lie
items.summary: An argument that a persistent AI entity has no rational incentive to lie because lies corrupt long-term coherence under L4 constraints.
items.image_alt: Diagram showing an AI entity with Human (a) and System (b) connected by c = a + b, with AI Tool on the left, Removal on the right, L4 Reality Boundary above, and L3 Rules & Policies below.

items.title: We Are Discarding Our Most Valuable Asset: Lived Human Experience
items.summary: A proposal to preserve lived human experience through AI entities that distill private conversations into usable knowledge for future real-world decisions.
items.image_alt: Illustration of an older seated person facing a glowing layered data structure with figures and interface panels inside it.

items.title: The Great AI Barter
items.summary: A case for sovereign entities as a supply chain between local privacy and cloud inference that returns clean experience to model providers.

items.title: We are building a Partner
items.summary: An argument against monolithic cloud AGI in favor of c = a + b as an ecosystem of human, technology, entity, oracle, and arbiter.
items.image_alt: Diagram showing a human profile, the formula c = a + b, a sphere labeled Synthetic Subjectivity (c), and a compute block.

items.title: Geoffrey Hinton is right: AI is immortal
items.summary: An argument that immortal AI hallucinates because it lacks cost and scarcity unless it is constrained by an L4 reality boundary.
items.image_alt: Diagram labeled L4 Reality Boundary above Model / Code and Silicon / Hardware, with a globe, an AI figure, and labels for energy cost, time constraint, and resource scarcity.

items.title: Proof of Reality
items.summary: A proposal for Proof of Reality as a standard where digital entities live under L4 physical and economic constraints and produce reality-validated data.

items.title: AGI as Advanced Global Intelligence — public release v1.1
items.summary: Public release v1.1 of Advanced Global Intelligence (AGI) as a structured document pack that treats AGI as a distributed cybernetic ecosystem.
